attributes.name: Brenda
attributes.notes: States they are 'just playing the doctor on tv'.
CT scan shows a stone measuring 0.5 centimeters; no evidence of hydronephrosis.
History of kidney stones; has passed stones on his own and has required surgery a couple of times. When passed on his own, usually within a week.
Pain score reported as 4/10 when worse; usually 1-2/10. One episode radiated to a testicle.
Calms the pain down a little bit but pain persists; doctor plans to continue ibuprofen and use for breakthrough pain.
Doctor to prescribe; prescription to be brought by nurse.
Discussed as possible further treatment if still symptomatic and stone persists; patient reports having had lithotripsy prior.

attributes.notes: Follow-up planned if still having symptoms and/or still has the stone; to discuss further treatment (possible lithotripsy).
Patient reports pain depends on how much he has been drinking.
Patient reports pain depends on what sort of activities he has been doing.

CT scan showed no evidence of hydronephrosis; nothing obstructing causing swelling in the kidney.
Doctor does not think Joshua will need any surgery for this kidney stone.

nodes.canonical_name: Weekend (time context for pain control/analgesia request)
